title: Utility-first CSS (Tailwind)
description: How atomic classes replace bespoke rules.

questions.prompt: The core idea of utility-first CSS:
options: One class per style — compose classes on markup
questions.explanation: Compose primitives (flex, p-4, text-sm) instead of naming components.

options: Apply on medium screens and above (min-width)
questions.explanation: Mobile-first breakpoints: sm, md, lg, xl, 2xl.

options: Long class strings vs shorter CSS files & fewer naming decisions
questions.explanation: Nothing is free — utility classes trade markup verbosity for less CSS.

questions.prompt: Dark mode in Tailwind (v4) is typically:
questions.explanation: You choose the strategy (media, class, data-attribute).

options: Extract utility combos into a real CSS rule when needed
questions.explanation: Handy for repeated combos or component styles.
